---
output: reprex::reprex_document
knit: reprex::reprex_render
---

Describe your issue very briefly here. Then show it with a minimal, self-contained example in the following R chunk.

```{r}
renv::init()      # Creates renv.lock + private library
renv::snapshot()  # Save current package state to renv.lock
renv::restore()   # On other machine: recreate exact environment
```


``` {r}

```
